title: zornia setosa subsp. obovata
intro: Zornia setosa subsp. obovata, also known as the hairy zornia, is a species of plant in the family Fabaceae. It is a herbaceous annual plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The hairy zornia is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. Traditionally, the plant can be prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, or used in infusions, to reap its numerous health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and promoting overall well-being.
